title: Solanum cundinamarcae
taxonomy: Solanum cundinamarcae is a species of flowering plant in the nightshade family, Solanaceae. It belongs to the genus Solanum and is part of the family's large and diverse group of plants. The plant is native to Colombia and is a type of tuberous solanum. It is classified as a species of plant within the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. The classification of Solanum cundinamarcae is also associated with the class Magnoliopsida.

intro: Solanum cundinamarcae, also known as Solanum cundinamarcanum, is a plant species in the nightshade family that is native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, contributing to overall cardiovascular health and well-being. Bioactive constituents of Solanum cundinamarcae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Solanum cundinamarcae is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies and folk medicines in the Andean region.
